message:
fix(core,db,server,share): BOCPD input validation, recovery hardening, SVG XSS prevention, snapshot PK fix

BOCPD (mcp-agent-mail-core):
- Clamp hazard rate to (0, 1) exclusive — 0.0 or 1.0 produce -inf log
  values that silently disable change-point detection
- Clamp NIG prior kappa/alpha/beta to positive values — zero or negative
  parameters cause division-by-zero or NaN in log_predictive
- Reject non-finite (NaN/Infinity) inputs in observe() — these would
  permanently corrupt the posterior, causing all future change points to
  be silently missed

Integrity recovery (mcp-agent-mail-db):
- Use PASSIVE checkpoint instead of TRUNCATE before recovery copy to
  avoid propagating WAL-resident corruption into the main database file
- Skip pre-recovery VACUUM that could partial-overwrite the original
  database before failing on corruption
- Copy WAL and SHM files alongside the main database for complete state
  preservation in recovery copies
- Distinguish genuinely empty rowsets (ok) from rows with no extractable
  text values (suspicious) in integrity check evaluation

Markdown sanitizer (mcp-agent-mail-server):
- Block data:image/svg data URIs in img src attributes — SVGs can
  contain embedded <script> tags, making them an XSS vector even when
  rendered as image sources

Snapshot PK fix (mcp-agent-mail-share):
- Include `kind` column in message_recipients primary key definition
  (was message_id + agent_id only, which dropped rows when an agent
  appears as both 'to' and 'cc' on the same message)
- Add test verifying snapshot preserves multiple recipient kinds for
  the same agent on the same message

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
